content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

March 22, 1985


William L. Cunningham
Education Advisor to the Governor
Governor's Office
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                      Re: Your Request for Advice
                                          Our File No. A-85-068

Dear Mr. Cunningham:

     Thank you for your request for advice concerning the
reporting duties of the members of the Commission for the Review
of the Master Plan for Higher Education under the Political
Reform Act./

                              QUESTION

     Does the Political Reform Act require the members of the
Commission for the Review of the Master Plan for Higher
Education to file Statements of Economic Interests.

                            CONCLUSION

     The Political Reform Act does not require the members of
the Commission for the Review of the Master Plan for Higher
Education to file Statements of Economic Interests.

                             DISCUSSION

     Education Code Section 66903.7, as added by Chapter 1507 of
the Statutes of 1984, establishes the Commission for Review
of the Master Plan for Higher Education. The Commission
consists of 16 appointed members. The members serve without

_____________
     1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
specified.

William L. Cunningham
March 22, 1985
Page 2


compensation but receive reimbursement for their actual and
necessary travel expenses. Education Code Section 66903.7(b).

The Commission for Review of the Master Plan for Higher
Education is required to review relevant reports submitted by
the California Postsecondary Education Commission, the
University of California, the California State University, the
Board of Governors of the California Community Colleges, the
Department of Finance, the Legislative Analyst, and any other
relevant reports. The Commission must also conduct public
hearings on, and formulate recommendations regarding,
the policies, content, goals, programs, functions, funding and
interrelationships of the Master Plan for Higher Education. The
Commission is required to submit a management plan for review by
the Joint Legislative Committee for Review of the Master Plan
for Higher Education by March 1, 1985, and must submit a report
to the Legislature and the Governor by January 1, 1986, relative
to its findings and recommendations. Education Code Section
66903.7(c) and (d). The Commission is required to select and
designate a state administrative agency to carry out the
personnel, contractual, and all other fiscal services required
by it, but has no authority to hire personnel. Education Code
Section 66903.7(c).

Pursuant to Education Code Section 66903.7(f), the
provisions establishing the Commission for the Review of the
Master Plan for Higher Education will become inoperative on
July 1, 1987, and will be repealed on January 1, 1988.

Section 87302 provides that public officials who
participate in or make decisions which may foreseeably have a
material effect on any financial interest should be "designated
employees" in a conflict of interest code. See also Section
82019. Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 defines
"public official" to include unsalaried members of boards which
make substantive recommendations which are, and over an
extended period of time, have been, regularly approved by
another public official or government agency. However, Section
82019(d) expressly excludes from the definition of "designated
employee" any unsalaried member of any board or commission which
serves a solely advisory function.

Applying these definitions, I conclude that the activities
of the Commission for the Review of the Master Plan for Higher
Education do not constitute governmental decisionmaking, and the
members of the Commission are not required to be "designated
employees." The Commission's actions are purely advisory, and
the Commission will remain in existence only for the relatively
short period of time that it must prepare and submit its report

William L. Cunningham
March 22, 1985
Page 3


and recommendations. Accordingly, the members of the Commission
for the Review of the Master Plan for Higher Education are not
required to file Statements of Economic Interests.

If you have any further questions about this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:plh

State of California

GOVERNOR'S OFFICE
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

[illegible] 14 11 11 ART J5

GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN
GOVERNOR

March 14, 1985


Mr. John Keplinger
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Keplinger:

RE: Applicability of FPPC Filing Requirements to Members of
the Commission for the Review of the Master Plan for
Higher Education

I respectfully request an opinion on the necessity for members of
the recently-constituted Commission for the Review of the Master
Plan for Higher Education (created by Chapter 1507, Statutes of
1984) to file economic disclosure statements. This opinion is
required prior to the first meeting of the Commission, scheduled
for March 25-26, 1985.

The Commission consists of sixteen members appointed by nine
different appointing powers. Its mandate is to review various
reports and plans relating to higher education in California and
to submit a report and recommendations to the Governor and
Legislature not later than January 1, 1987, after which time it
will expire. The Commission is strictly advisory and has no
regulatory or program implementation responsibilities. A copy of
the statute creating the Commission is attached.

Because the first meeting will be held very shortly, I will
appreciate your giving this request your immediate attention. If
you need any additional information, please call Carol Gates at
323-0611.

Sincerely,

William L. Cunningham
Education Adviser to the Governor

Attachment

Education to file Statement,of Economic Interests.
sections.conclusion:
The Political Reform Act does not require the members of 
the Commission for the Review of the Master Plan for Higher 
Education to file Statements of Economic Interests.
